name: RLDS
desc:
**RLDS (Reinforcement Learning Datasets Standard)** is a standardized robot learning data format proposed by Google DeepMind, built on the TFDS framework.

Organizes data into the **episode → step → observation/action/reward** hierarchical structure, supporting efficient random access and streaming loading.

Serves as the underlying format foundation for Open X-Embodiment and has been adopted by **60+ datasets**, becoming the de facto data format standard in the embodied AI field.

name: Open X-Embodiment Schema
desc:
**Open X-Embodiment Schema** is the cross-robot unified data schema defined by the Open X-Embodiment project.

Extends RLDS with standardized field mappings across **22 robot morphologies**.

The core contribution is mapping heterogeneous robot sensors and action spaces into a unified observation/action namespace, making cross-embodiment joint training possible.

name: LeRobot Format
desc:
**LeRobot** is a lightweight robot learning data format launched by Hugging Face.

Uses **Parquet / Apache Arrow** as the storage backend, supporting efficient memory-mapped reading.

Provides Python API and visualization tools, emphasizing ease of use and deep integration with the Hugging Face Hub ecosystem. Already adopted by AGI Odyssey, SimplerEnv, and other projects.

name: HDF5 机器人数据格式
desc:
**HDF5 Robomimic** is an HDF5-based robot manipulation data format proposed by Stanford / AI2, specifically designed for imitation learning.

Organizes demonstrations as **state/action/reward/done quadruples**, supporting multiple observation modalities (RGB, depth, point cloud, proprioception).

Serves as the default format for the robomimic framework and is used by BridgeData V2, RoboTurk, and other datasets.

name: MCAP
desc:
**MCAP (Message Capture)** is an open-source robot logging format developed by Foxglove.

Supports **multi-topic, multi-timeline** structured message recording with random access.

Designed to replace ROS bag, supporting protobuf, JSON, CBOR, and other serialization formats. Widely adopted by ROS 2, Foxglove Studio, and other tools.

name: OpenUSD
desc:
**OpenUSD (Universal Scene Description)** is a general-purpose 3D scene description format developed by Pixar and championed by NVIDIA.

Organizes 3D scene geometry, materials, lighting, and physical properties through a **Layer / Stage / Prim** hierarchy.

NVIDIA Isaac Sim builds robot simulation data pipelines on OpenUSD, supporting large-scale parallel scene generation and domain randomization.

name: ROS 2 消息标准
desc:
**ROS 2** is the robot middleware released by Open Robotics, defining standardized message serialization and communication standards.

Uses **.msg / .srv / .action** to define normalized message interfaces (sensors, control commands, states, etc.), supporting DDS real-time communication.

Serves as the de facto standard for robot data collection and playback; rosbag2 can directly record in MCAP format.

name: CALVIN
desc:
**CALVIN Benchmark** is a language-conditioned long-sequence robot manipulation benchmark proposed by ETH Zurich.

Defines a standardized **task definition format, evaluation protocol, and data splits** (ABCD four-fold), testing policy generalization on unseen environments, unseen instructions, and unseen objects.

Built on PyBullet simulation, providing 20,000+ teleoperation demonstration trajectories.

name: LIBERO
desc:
**LIBERO** is a lifelong robot learning benchmark proposed by Stanford / AI2.

Defines **130 language-conditioned manipulation tasks** across **5 generalization dimensions** (scene, object, layout, task composition, unseen tasks).

Data released in HDF5 format, providing 50+ hours of human teleoperation demonstrations with continual learning evaluation support.

name: RLBench
desc:
**RLBench** is a robot manipulation benchmark framework proposed by the University of Oxford.

Defines **100 language-described manipulation tasks** in CoppeliaSim, supporting procedural task generation and variant control.

Rigorously evaluates policy generalization through task variation dimensions (position, pose, color, shape, etc.).

name: MetaWorld
desc:
**MetaWorld** is a multi-task robot manipulation benchmark proposed by UC Berkeley.

Defines **50 tabletop manipulation tasks** (cabinet opening, button pressing, object pushing, etc.) in MuJoCo, built on the Gymnasium API.

Tasks are parameterized — the same task can generate variants with different positions and goals — supporting multi-task and meta-learning evaluation.

name: ManiSkill2
desc:
**ManiSkill2** is a general-purpose robot manipulation benchmark proposed by Peking University.

Built in the SAPIEN engine with **2,000+ objects and 4,000+ task variants**, supporting rigid-body, articulated, and fluid manipulation.

Features GPU-parallel rendering for accelerated data collection and a novel task generation pipeline that automatically produces large volumes of diverse training data.

name: BEHAVIOR-1K
desc:
**BEHAVIOR-1K** is an ultra-large-scale daily living task benchmark proposed by Stanford, Duke, and others.

Defines **1,000 home activity tasks** (cleaning, cooking, organizing, etc.) in the OmniGibson simulation.

Formally describes task goals and predicate logic using **BDDL (BEHAVIOR Domain Definition Language)**, testing long-term planning and physical interaction capabilities.

name: Habitat
desc:
**Habitat** is an embodied navigation and interaction simulation platform standard proposed by Meta AI.

Defines standardized scene formats (**GLB/glTF**), task configurations (**JSON**), and sensor specifications.

Supports Matterport3D, Gibson, HM3D and other 3D scene datasets, and hosts the annual Habitat Challenge benchmark.

name: 中国标准体系 (2026版)
fullName: 人形机器人与具身智能标准体系
desc:
**China Embodied AI Data Standard System** is under development under the guidance of China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology.

Aims to establish unified **robot dataset description specifications, quality evaluation metrics, and benchmark standards**.

First included in the government work report in 2026, marking the elevation of embodied AI data standardization to the national strategic level. Currently in the framework design phase; specific standard texts have not yet been published.

name: 上海数据集标准
fullName: 人形机器人数据集标准 / 浦江X
org: 上海机器人产业技术研究院
desc:
**Shanghai Robot Industry Data Standard** is a local robot dataset standard released by the Shanghai Robot Industry Technology Research Institute.

Addresses the data collection, annotation, and sharing needs of the Yangtze River Delta embodied AI industry with standardized **dataset description templates and quality control processes**.

Serves as the first local government embodied AI data standard, already being piloted in select enterprises.

name: ISO/TC 299
fullName: 机器人技术委员会国际标准
desc:
**ISO/TC 299 (Robotics Technical Committee)** is responsible for developing international standards in the robotics field.

Covers **industrial robot safety (ISO 10218), service robot safety (ISO 13482), robot modularity (ISO 22166)**, and more.

The work of its subcommittees WG 3 (Service Robots) and WG 4 (Industrial Robots) is closely related to embodied AI data standardization.

name: DROID
desc:
**DROID Protocol** is the standardized data collection protocol and format specification accompanying the DROID project.

Defines the unified Franka Panda hardware platform's **sensor configuration (3-view RGB-D), teleoperation interface, and RLDS-compatible data schema**.

The 18-institution joint collection follows this protocol, ensuring consistency across large-scale multi-scene data.

name: AgiBot World 数据规范
fullName: 全域真实场景百万级真机数据集格式
desc:
**AgiBot Data Standard** is a large-scale real robot manipulation data specification released by AgiBot.

Built on the in-house embodied AI data collection platform, defining **multi-sensor alignment (vision/tactile/force control/proprioception) and task-level annotation format**, covering home, industrial, and other scenarios.

Data released in RLDS-compatible format, supporting cross-platform joint training.
